last_completed_round.direction_name: Keep the peeled steady state, but restore the proven final-two-tile handoff to recover correctness
last_completed_round.hypothesis: Round 4 produced the clearest positive runtime signal in many rounds: the dominant 256x128 hot-band kernel dropped from 41.080 ms to 40.365 ms and tensor activity rose from 38.75% to 39.28%. That means the fixed-shape steady-state peeling direction is probably correct, but the manual epilogue around the last two K-tiles is not. Instead of abandoning the direction, the next move should keep the branch-free steady-state core and hand the last two iterations back to the original, already-correct next/future/wait0 control sequence. This preserves almost all of the control-flow win while removing the highest-risk cp.async boundary change.
